file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)
input.new_string:
# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a 16-digit credit card number, grouped into four sets of four
# digits. The separator between groups (none, space, or hyphen) must be
# used consistently throughout the number.
credit_card = Regex(r"\d{4}([ -]?)\d{4}\1\d{4}\1\d{4}")

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Now let's verify it works as expected.

input.command:
python -c "
import re
from outlines.types import credit_card
p = credit_card.pattern
print(p)
tests = {
  '1234-5678-9012-3456': True,
  '1234 5678 9012 3456': True,
  '1234567890123456': True,
  '1234-5678 9012-3456': False,
  '123456789012345': False,
}
for t, expected in tests.items():
    m = re.fullmatch(p, t)
    print(t, bool(m), 'OK' if bool(m) == expected else 'FAIL')
"

python -c "
import re
from outlines.types import credit_card
p = credit_card.pattern
print(p)
tests = {
  '1234-5678-9012-3456': True,
  '1234 5678 9012 3456': True,
  '1234567890123456': True,
  '1234-5678 9012-3456': False,
  '123456789012345': False,
}
for t, expected in tests.items():
    m = re.fullmatch(p, t)
    print(t, bool(m), 'OK' if bool(m) == expected else 'FAIL')
"

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import credit_card
from pydantic import BaseModel

class PaymentForm(BaseModel):
    card_number: credit_card

form = PaymentForm(card_number='1234-5678-9012-3456')
print(form)
try:
    PaymentForm(card_number='123456789012345')
    print('should have failed')
except Exception as e:
    print('correctly raised:', type(e).__name__)
"

content.content:
"""
    match term:
        case String():
            return re.escape(term.value)
        case Regex():
            return f"({term.pattern})"
        case JsonSchema():
            regex_str = build_regex_from_schema(term.schema, term.whitespace_pattern)
            return f"({regex_str})"
        case KleeneStar():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)})*"
        case KleenePlus():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)})+"
        case Optional():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)})?"
        case Alternatives():
            regexes = [to_regex(subterm) for subterm in term.terms]
            return f"({'|'.join(regexes)})"
        case Sequence():
            regexes = [to_regex(subterm) for subterm in term.terms]
            return f"{''.join(regexes)}"
        case QuantifyExact():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{{term.count}}}"
        case QuantifyMinimum():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{{term.min_count},}}"
        case QuantifyMaximum():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{,{term.max_count}}}"
        case QuantifyBetween():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{{term.min_count},{term.max_count}}}"
        case _:
            raise TypeError(
                f"Cannot convert object {repr(term)} to a regular expression."
            )

    match term:

            return f"({regex_str})"
        case KleeneStar():

            return f"({to_regex(term.term)})?"

            regexes = [to_regex(subterm) for subterm in term.terms]
            return f"({'|'.join(regexes)})"
        case Sequence():
            regexes = [to_regex(subterm) for subterm in term.terms]
            return f"{''.join(regexes)}"
        case QuantifyExact():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{{term.count}}}"
        case QuantifyMinimum():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{{term.min_count},}}"
        case QuantifyMaximum():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{,{term.max_count}}}"
        case QuantifyBetween():
            return f"({to_regex(term.term)}){{{term.min_count},{term.max_count}}}"
        case _:
            raise TypeError(
                f"Cannot convert object {repr(term)} to a regular expression."
            )

# Matches a 16-digit credit card number, grouped into four sets of four
# digits. The separator between groups (none, space, or hyphen) must be
# used consistently throughout the number.
credit_card = Regex(r"\d{4}([ -]?)\d{4}\1\d{4}\1\d{4}")
input.new_string:
# Matches a 16-digit credit card number, grouped into four sets of four
# digits, with no separators, space separators, or hyphen separators (the
# separator must be used consistently throughout the number).
credit_card = Regex(r"(?:\d{4}){4}|\d{4}(?:-\d{4}){3}|\d{4}(?: \d{4}){3}")

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a 16-digit credit card number, grouped into four sets of four
# digits. The separator between groups (none, space, or hyphen) must be
# used consistently throughout the number.
credit_card = Regex(r"\d{4}([ -]?)\d{4}\1\d{4}\1\d{4}")

lines: # Matches a 16-digit credit card number, grouped into four sets of four
-# digits. The separator between groups (none, space, or hyphen) must be
+# digits, with no separators, space separators, or hyphen separators (the
+# separator must be used consistently throughout the number).

input.command:
python -c "
import re
from outlines.types import credit_card
from pydantic import BaseModel

class PaymentForm(BaseModel):
    card_number: credit_card

tests = {
  '1234-5678-9012-3456': True,
  '1234 5678 9012 3456': True,
  '1234567890123456': True,
  '1234-5678 9012-3456': False,
  '123456789012345': False,
}
for t, expected in tests.items():
    try:
        PaymentForm(card_number=t)
        ok = True
    except Exception:
        ok = False
    print(t, ok, 'OK' if ok == expected else 'FAIL')
"

content.text: The `credit_card` regex type has been added to `outlines/types/__init__.py`, matching 16-digit card numbers with no separator, or consistent space/hyphen separators. Verified via pydantic model validation (valid and invalid cases both behave correctly, including the wrapping-group backreference pitfall I caught and fixed by switching to explicit alternation instead of a backreference). Confirmed with agent2 (working in an isolated copy, adding `hash_sha256`) that there's no file collision. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
